task: Browse the class schedule of graduate-level computer science courses.
thoughts: I can see the Ohio State University homepage. Let me first accept the cookies and then look for the class schedule. I'll click "Accept cookies" first.

Good, the cookie banner is dismissed. Now I need to find the class schedule. Let me look for it through the "Academics" menu or the "Current Students" menu which likely has a link to the class schedule.

I'm on the Current Students page. I can see links for Undergraduate and Graduate students. Let me scroll down to see if there's a direct link to class schedules, or I should look for it through the search. Let me scroll down to see more content on this page.
I can see the Resources section with various links. I need to find the class schedule. Let me use the search function to search for "class schedule" directly. Let me click on the search icon.
A search box appeared. Let me type "class schedule" to search for it.
A search box appeared. Let me type "class schedule" to search for it.
A search box appeared. Let me type "class schedule" to search for it.
I see search results are loading. Let me scroll down to see the website results which should have a link to the class schedule.

I can see website results. The first few results are from the registrar. Let me scroll down a bit more to see if there's a direct "class schedule" link, or I can try navigating to the registrar's site directly. Let me scroll down to see more results.
I can see "Class, catalog, and space - The Ohio State University" from registrar.osu.edu which seems like the right place to find class schedules. Let me click on that link.
I'm on the University Registrar's "Class, catalog, and space" page. I can see "Schedule of Classes" section. Let me first accept the cookies and then click on "Schedule of Classes".
The cookie banner is still showing. Let me click "Accept cookies" more precisely.
Let me close the cookie banner by clicking the X button, then proceed to the Schedule of Classes.
The cookie banner is still persistent. Let me scroll down to bypass it and click on "Schedule of Classes" which I can partially see.
Now I can see the "Schedule of Classes" section with a "Search Schedule of Classes" link. Let me click on that to find the class schedule search tool.
I see the "Search Schedule of Classes" link. Let me click directly on that text link.
The click didn't seem to navigate. Let me try clicking more precisely on the "Search Schedule of Classes" link text.

Now I can see the clickable elements highlighted in green. I can see "Search Schedule of Classes" is a clickable link. The cookie banner might be intercepting my clicks. Let me first dismiss the cookie banner by clicking the X button, then click the link.
The cookie banner is now dismissed. Now I can clearly see the "Search Schedule of Classes" link. Let me click on it.
Let me navigate directly to that URL.

I'm now on the Class Search page. I can see search criteria including Institution (The Ohio State University), Term (Spring 2026), Campus, Subject, Course Number, and Course Career. I need to:

1. Select "Columbus" campus
2. Select "Computer Science & Engineering" or "CSE" as the Subject
3. Select "Graduate" as the Course Career

Let me start by selecting the Campus dropdown. Let me click on it first.
I see the Campus dropdown opened with a select picker. Let me select "Columbus" (index 1).
Columbus is now selected. Now I need to select the Subject. Let me click on the Subject dropdown.
I can see the Subject dropdown was opened and I found "Computer Science & Engineering" at index 61. Let me select it.
Now I need to select "Graduate" as the Course Career. Let me click on the Course Career dropdown.
I can see the Course Career dropdown opened with "Graduate" at index 2. Let me select it.

All the search criteria are set correctly:
- Institution: The Ohio State University
- Term: Spring 2026
- Campus: Columbus
- Subject: Computer Science & Engineering
- Course Career: Graduate

Now let me uncheck "Show Open Classes Only" to see all graduate CS courses, then click Search.
"Show Open Classes Only" is now unchecked. Everything is set. Now let me click the "Search" button.

I see an error message: "Your search will exceed the maximum limit of 300 sections. Specify additional criteria to continue." I need to narrow down the search. Let me check the "Show Open Classes Only" box back, or I can add a course number range. Let me re-check the "Show Open Classes Only" checkbox to narrow the results.

Good, "Show Open Classes Only" is now checked again. Let me also change the Course Number filter from "is exactly" to a range. Let me change it to "greater than or equal to" and enter a course number. Actually, let me try using the course number range. Let me first change the "is exactly" dropdown to see options.

I see the course number options. Let me select "greater than or equal to" and enter a course number like 5000 (graduate courses at OSU are typically numbered 5000+). This should help narrow down the results.
